MINI MINUTES: RE: PLAINTIFFS MARY GEAN (INDIVIDUALLY AND AS TRUSTEE OF THE MARY M. GEAN TRUST), JULUNDA GEAN, TEDMUND GEAN, and DESMUND GEAN'S Motion For Order Deeming Requests For Admissions Be Admitted And Awarding Monetary Sanctions.

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person.

The court adopts its tentative ruling. Motion GRANTED in part. Defendant must revise the responses to RFA 34 and 35. CCP 2033.220(c) requires that a statement of inability must include information about a reasonable inquiry. Sanctions GRANTED in the amount of $980 for 1 hour of attorney fees at $650 plus 1 hour of law clerk fees at $250, plus the motion filing fee and service costs. . Sanctions are mandatory under CCP 2033.280(c). This reduction is based on the fact that Defendant consistently requested extensions based on medical issues and ultimately produced the responses before the hearing, so an additional extension could have addressed the issues. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem to sborrowman@gmail.com.

MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-18-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Jethro S. Busch; Steven Adair MacDonald & Partners, P.C.; 415-956-6488; appearing as counsel for plaitniffs and cross-defendants Mary Gean, Julunda Gean, Tedmund Gean, and Desmund Gean.
Melinda Gean; defendant and cross-complainant, appearing in pro per.
John V. Grienauer; 310-373-5944; appearing as counsel for defendant and cross-complainant Edmund Gean.

Mr. Busch contests the court's tentative decision. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

CROSS-DEFENDANTS MARY GEAN, JULUNDA GEAN, TEDMUND GEAN AND DESMUND GEAN'S MOTION TO STRIKE AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion is granted as to (1) the first two sentences of paragraph 8; and (2) the following part of paragraph 34: "Cross-Defendant Tedmund assaulted her on one occasion at the Property," Motion is otherwise denied. Moving party failed to carry its burden to show that these parts of the cross-complaint are irrelevant, improper and not drawn in conformity with the law. The Court notes that the Notice of Motion and the Points and Authorities do not discuss identical parts of the cross-complaint purportedly subject to the motion. For example, paragraphs 39 and 45 are discussed at length in the Points and Authorities, but are not listed in the Notice of Motion. Paragraph 16 and 17 of the complaint and various paragraphs of the prayer are listed in the Notice of Motion, but are not discussed in the Points and Authorities.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)
